title: Agent Olympics Round Manifest
description: A round manifest describes one competition round: which tasks from a season are selected, which participants are invited, deterministic run IDs and directory layout, and the round lifecycle state. Rounds are source-only artifacts — the runner orchestrates actual execution via the round CLI.

season.description: The competition season this round belongs to.

description.description: Optional longer description of the round's purpose, variant seed, or special conditions.

status.description: Current lifecycle state of this round.

runner.description: Identifier of the runner/adapter that orchestrated this round.

runner_version.description: Runner software version used for orchestration.

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle tracking for the round. The runner transitions status as orchestration proceeds.

task_id.description: Task identifier matching a Season 001 fixture and envelope.

title.description: Human-readable task title (copied from the envelope for convenience).

envelope_path.description: Path to the task envelope YAML file (v2 preferred for participant-facing rounds).

time_limit_minutes.description: Time limit for this task in minutes.

fixture_bundle_ref.description: Path to the fixture bundle directory for this task.

oracle_ref.description: Private path to the oracle/answer key file for this task (judge only).

judge_notes_ref.description: Private path to judge notes for this task (judge only).

scoring_rubric.description: Path to the scoring rubric (overrides envelope default if set).

order.description: Execution order for sequential rounds. Omitted or equal order means parallel.

tasks.description: List of tasks selected for this round, referencing Season 001 fixtures.

model.description: Optional model identifier used by this participant.

node.description: Optional node identifier where this participant runs.

enabled.description: If false, skip this participant without removing from the manifest.

participants.description: List of participants competing in this round.

run_directory.description: Base directory for run artifacts. Typically 'runs/<season>/<round_id>/'.

run_id_template.description: Template for deterministic run ID generation. Variables: {task_id}, {agent_id}, {timestamp}, {round_id}, {season}.

parent_issue.description: Link to the parent coordination issue for this round.

round_issue.description: Link to the round-specific tracking issue.

metadata.description: Extensible metadata bag for runner-specific or season-specific fields.
